what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline bell icon using currentColor stroke and no fill.
observations: The root svg uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with width and height set to 24.
The icon is drawn with two path elements.
The first path draws the bell body, top arc, side lines, bottom edge, and lower points.
The second path draws the bottom clapper or ringing base curve.
The SVG uses fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5", stroke-linecap="round", and stroke-linejoin="round".
standouts: Uses currentColor so the icon color inherits from surrounding text or CSS.
The full icon geometry is contained inline in path data.
conventions: Follows a common 24px outline icon convention.
Uses rounded stroke caps and joins for soft outline rendering.
Keeps icon styling on the svg element rather than per-path styling.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present, so accessibility semantics must come from the consuming context.
notes: A file-local SVG asset for a stroked notification bell icon.
questions: Should this icon be decorative or exposed with an accessible label when used?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a bell icon, and the SVG content draws a bell-shaped outline icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable notification bell icon whose color can be controlled by surrounding UI styles.
contribution: Contributes a currentColor, 24px outline notification bell asset that a fused system would keep as a reusable icon substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG outline bell icon made from two rounded stroke paths.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing notifications or alerts as a reusable themeable bell icon asset.
